name: Nogarejas from Pinilla de la Valdería — circular by the Castillo de Nogarejas
name_original: Nogarejas desde Pinilla de la Valdería — circular por el Castillo de Nogarejas
region_intro: Hiking around Castrocontrigo offers a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration. The region features a mix of valleys, forested areas, and historic sites, providing varied terrain for hikers. Trails often lead through natural settings and past cultural landmarks like castles and reservoirs. The area's topography includes gentle ascents and descents, making it suitable for different levels of hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Castrocontrigo The most popular hiking route is Nogarejas from Pinilla de la Valdería — circular by the Castillo de Nogarejas, a 14.4 miles (23.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a notable castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo, a moderate 9.9 miles (15.9 km) path. This trail offers views of the Embalse de Valtabuyo reservoir. Local hikers also love the Castrocalbón Circular Route via the Castle and, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Hiking around Castrocontrigo is defined by valleys, reservoirs, and historic sites, offering options for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 2 hikers have used komoot to explore Castrocontrigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Castrocontrigo?
A: There are 5 hiking trails documented around Castrocontrigo, offering a range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Castrocontrigo offers easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271723059/castrocalbon-circular-route-via-the-castle-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrocalbón Circular Route via the Castle and&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.9 km path, typically completed in just over an hour. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valtabuyo Reservoir loop from Priaranza de la Valduerna&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 km long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Castrocontrigo?
A: The region around Castrocontrigo features a diverse landscape, including valleys, forested areas, and historic sites. Trails often lead through natural settings with gentle ascents and descents, making them suitable for various experience levels. You'll encounter a mix of natural paths and sometimes cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For those seeking a more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271771438/nogarejas-from-pinilla-de-la-valderia-circular-by-the-castillo-de-nogarejas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogarejas from Pinilla de la Valdería — circular by the Castillo de Nogarejas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.2 km trail that takes approximately 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features a notable castle and offers a significant challenge.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Castrocontrigo?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271723059/castrocalbon-circular-route-via-the-castle-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrocalbón Circular Route via the Castle and&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271912300/tabuyo-del-monte-circular-by-the-embalse-de-valtabuyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 15.9 km loop with reservoir views.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking in Castrocontrigo often brings you close to historical and cultural landmarks. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6039414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrocontrigo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eria Bridge in Castrocalbón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5510280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gothic Arch of the Palace of Castrocalbón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5452845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Castrocontrigo&lt;/a&gt; is another notable religious building in the area.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies like reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region includes routes with views of water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271912300/tabuyo-del-monte-circular-by-the-embalse-de-valtabuyo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabuyo del Monte — circular by the Embalse de Valtabuyo&lt;/a&gt; trail offers scenic views of the Embalse de Valtabuyo reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valtabuyo Reservoir loop from Priaranza de la Valduerna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Castrocontrigo?
A: Hike durations vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271723059/castrocalbon-circular-route-via-the-castle-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrocalbón Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while longer, difficult trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271771438/nogarejas-from-pinilla-de-la-valderia-circular-by-the-castillo-de-nogarejas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogarejas from Pinilla de la Valdería&lt;/a&gt; can take over six hours.

Q: Are there any trails that highlight unique natural features like ancient trees?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305973560/ancient-trees-of-las-majadas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Trees of Las Majadas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7 km route that specifically showcases the impressive ancient trees of the area, offering a unique natural experience.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Castrocontrigo?
A: The diverse landscape of Castrocontrigo makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be enjoyable, especially in forested areas. Winter hiking is also possible, though conditions may vary and require appropriate gear.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead may be limited in this rural region, it's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Castrocontrigo or nearby villages. Many visitors prefer to use personal vehicles for greater flexibility in accessing the various starting points.

Q: What should I consider regarding parking for trails near Castrocontrigo?
A: Parking availability can vary by trailhead. For popular routes or those starting in villages, you can often find designated parking areas or roadside spots. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a convenient parking space. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.
tips: A small hermitage on the outskirts of the town of Nogarejas along the Ería river valley. The hermitage is surrounded by views of the mountains of Nogarejas.
Ascending the route towards the medieval castle of Nogarejas watching over the Ería river valley. There isn't much left but the watchtower.
